merged_events.event_description: American Legation established in Vienna when Chargé d'Affaires Arthur Hugh Frazier presented his credentials.

evidence.quote: Arthur Hugh Frazier was appointed Chargé d'Affaires to Austria and presented his credentials on November 26, 1921.

merged_events.event_description: Following the Anschluss, the U.S. Legation in Vienna was closed and converted to a Consulate General (coded as Consulate, being in the capital).

evidence.quote: He closed the Legation on April 30, 1938, and it became a Consulate General, a step that Secretary of State Cordell Hull noted had to be taken "as a practical measure."

merged_events.event_description: The Vienna Consulate General was closed, ending all U.S. diplomatic representation in Austria.

evidence.quote: The Vienna Consulate General was closed July 9, 1941, along with all other U.S. consulates in Germany.

merged_events.event_description: John G. Erhardt was appointed as U.S. Political Representative to the Austrian Government, establishing a liaison-level presence.

evidence.quote: John G. Erhardt was appointed as the U.S. Political Representative to the Austrian Government on January 21, 1946.

merged_events.event_description: Erhardt presented his credentials as Minister to Austria, re-establishing the American Legation in Vienna.

evidence.quote: He was appointed Minister to Austria on August 3. He presented his credentials on September 7, and served until June 27, 1950.

merged_events.event_description: The American Legation was elevated to Embassy status when Minister Walter J. Donnelly was promoted to Ambassador.

evidence.quote: American Minister Walter J. Donnelly was promoted to Ambassador on November 16, 1951. He presented his credentials on November 28, and served until July 19, 1952.

merged_events.event_description: Henry Augustus Philip Muhlenberg presents credentials as the first Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Austria, establishing a U.S. legation.

evidence.quote: Henry Augustus Philip Muhlenberg, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1838-02-08. Credentials presented: 1838-11-07. Left post on 1840-09-18.

merged_events.event_description: John Lothrop Motley presents credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, elevating the U.S. mission from a legation to an embassy.

evidence.quote: John Lothrop Motley, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1861-08-10. Credentials presented: 1861-11-14. Left post: 1867-06-14.

merged_events.event_description: Henry Miller Watts presents credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, reverting the U.S. mission from embassy to legation status.

evidence.quote: Henry Miller Watts, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1868-07-25. Credentials presented: 1868-09-25. Presented recall on 1869-06-01.

merged_events.event_description: Robert Sanderson McCormick is promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, elevating the U.S. mission from legation to embassy.

evidence.quote: Robert Sanderson Mccormick, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1901-03-07. Credentials presented: 1901-04-29. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary on 1902-06-26.

merged_events.event_description: Austria-Hungary severs relations with the United States, ending all formal U.S. diplomatic representation.

evidence.quote: Joseph Clark Grew, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1913-04-07. Austria-Hungary severed relations with the United States on 1917-04-09.

merged_events.event_description: Arthur Hugh Frazier is received as Chargé d'Affaires pro tempore on an informal, provisional basis without a formal commission, establishing a liaison-level presence.

evidence.quote: Arthur Hugh Frazier, Chargé d'Affaires pro tempore. Credentials presented: 1921-11-26. Left post on 1922-05-21. Note: Not commissioned; letter of credence dated November 25, 1921. Recognized provisionally as Charge d'Affaires pro tempore and received as such on November 26, 1921.

merged_events.event_description: Albert Henry Washburn presents credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, re-establishing a formal U.S. legation in Austria.

evidence.quote: Albert Henry Washburn, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1922-02-10. Credentials presented: 1922-06-19. Died at post on 1930-04-29.

merged_events.event_description: The U.S. legation in Austria is closed following Austria's annexation by Germany on March 13, 1938, ending all formal U.S. diplomatic representation.

evidence.quote: John Cooper Wiley, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1937-09-26. Legation closed on 1938-04-30. Note: Austria was annexed to Germany on March 13, 1938.

merged_events.event_description: Walter Joseph Donnelly is promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and presents credentials, elevating the U.S. mission from legation to embassy.

evidence.quote: Walter Joseph Donnelly, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1950-09-20. Credentials presented: 1950-10-25. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary on 1951-11-28.

matched.notes: RDCR Event 0 (1921-11-26, Legation) is the primary match. POCOM Event 11 characterizes this as Liaison (provisional Chargé without commission), and POCOM Event 12 (1922-06-19) marks the formal legation with a commissioned Minister. RDCR explicitly states the Legation was established on 1921-11-26, so RDCR wins per source priority. CSV date and status match RDCR.

RDCR Event 1 (1938-04-30, Consulate) matches CSV. POCOM Event 13 records this as 'None' (legation closed), but RDCR clarifies the legation became a Consulate General on the same date. RDCR wins. Note: Event 1 has a CITATION ERROR flag, but the substance is consistent with the described event and POCOM confirms the April 30 date for the legation closure.
Perfect match. RDCR Event 2 confirms the Vienna Consulate General was closed on July 9, 1941.
RDCR Event 4 confirms Erhardt presented credentials as Minister on September 7, 1946, re-establishing the Legation. CSV matches exactly.

RDCR Event 5 gives November 16, 1951 (promotion date) while POCOM Event 14 gives November 28, 1951 (credentials presented after promotion). The RDCR date is a personnel/promotion date, not an institutional date, so per source priority rules POCOM's date wins. CSV uses 1951-11-28, matching POCOM — correct.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Henry Augustus Philip Muhlenberg presented credentials as the first Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Austria, establishing the initial U.S. legation.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. This is the original establishment of U.S. diplomatic relations with Austria (then the Austrian Empire). The CSV appears to begin coverage only from the post-WWI re-establishment in 1921, omitting the entire 19th-century and early 20th-century diplomatic history.

missing_from_csv.event_description: John Lothrop Motley presented credentials as Ambassador, elevating the U.S. mission from legation to embassy.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. Elevation to embassy during the Civil War era. Not covered in CSV which starts at 1921.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Henry Miller Watts presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, reverting the mission from embassy back to legation.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. Reversion from embassy to legation status. Not covered in CSV.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Robert Sanderson McCormick promoted to Ambassador, elevating the mission from legation to embassy.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. Second elevation to embassy status. Not covered in CSV.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Austria-Hungary severed relations with the United States, ending all formal U.S. diplomatic representation.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. This is the break in relations during WWI that preceded the 1921 re-establishment. Important predecessor event to CSV Row 1.

missing_from_csv.event_description: John G. Erhardt appointed as U.S. Political Representative to the Austrian Government, establishing a liaison-level presence before the formal re-establishment of the Legation.
missing_from_csv.notes: From RDCR. This is a distinct event preceding the Legation re-establishment (CSV Row 4, 1946-09-07). Erhardt served as Political Representative from January 1946 before being appointed Minister in August and presenting credentials in September. This interim liaison status is not captured in the CSV.
